sections.subtitle: Recent Form
sections.text: Real Sociedad have struggled for wins, drawing their last competitive match 1-1 against Valencia and failing to secure a victory in their last 3 games. Espanyol, on the other hand, are in good form with a 2-1 win over Atlético Madrid in their last match, showing resilience and attacking intent.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: Real Sociedad have dominated recent encounters with Espanyol, winning all of their last 5 head-to-head matches, including a 2-1 victory at home in February 2025 and a 1-0 win away in August 2024. This historical edge suggests they are likely to control the game.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Real Sociedad's season stats show a balanced but underwhelming attack with only 1 goal scored and a low conversion rate of big chances (0%). Espanyol, with 2 goals from limited opportunities, appear slightly more clinical despite similar possession and chance creation stats.

sections.subtitle: Defensive and Disciplinary Trends
sections.text: Both teams have shown solid defensive stats with Real Sociedad conceding just 1 goal and Espanyol matching that record. Yellow card averages are low for both (2 per game), and with the referee averaging 5.2 cards per game, a moderate number of bookings is expected.

sections.subtitle: Set-Piece and Corner Analysis
sections.text: Real Sociedad average 7 corners per game, ranking high in the league, while Espanyol have fewer (as per recent matches). Head-to-head data indicates matches between these teams often see less than 10.5 corners, suggesting a tight game in this aspect.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, Espanyol leads Real Sociedad 2-0 at the Reale Arena in San Sebastian. Espanyol struck early with a headed goal in the 10th minute and doubled their advantage just before the break with a penalty. Real Sociedad, despite dominating possession and creating opportunities, failed to convert their chances and find the back of the net.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Real Sociedad's first half performance
halftimeSections.text: Real Sociedad controlled much of the game in terms of possession and showed intent by creating a couple of big chances. However, their finishing let them down as they missed key opportunities. Their high number of final third and penalty area entries indicate a strong presence in attacking zones, but the lack of clinical edge has left them trailing. Defensively, they held firm for most of the half but were caught out early and again late with the penalty concession.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Espanyol's first half performance
halftimeSections.text: Espanyol has been clinical in front of goal, capitalizing on their big chances to secure a two-goal cushion. Their attacking output has been efficient, converting opportunities with precision while maintaining a balanced defensive structure. Despite having less of the ball, their ability to transition quickly and exploit defensive lapses has been the difference. They've also shown resilience in duels, narrowly edging out Real Sociedad in overall physicality.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw Real Sociedad start with strong attacking intent, pushing forward with consistent spells of pressure in the opening minutes. However, Espanyol shifted the momentum around the time of their first goal, gaining confidence and creating dangerous moments. Real Sociedad regained some control mid-half with sustained attacking phases, but Espanyol took over late in the half, especially leading up to their second goal. The momentum swung back and forth, with both teams having periods of dominance in the opposition's half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Real Sociedad, players like Takefusa Kubo and Ander Barrenetxea have shown promise in creating chances and will be crucial if they are to mount a comeback. Mikel Oyarzabal, despite missing a big chance, remains a threat upfront. On the Espanyol side, Javi Puado has been lethal, already finding the net from the penalty spot, while Pere Milla's early goal highlights his danger in the box. Roberto Fernández's assist and involvement in build-up play also make him a player to monitor.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Real Sociedad will likely come out with renewed vigor, pushing hard to reduce the deficit early in the second half. Their superior possession and attacking entries suggest they can create more chances, but they must be more clinical. Espanyol, on the other hand, will aim to protect their lead, potentially sitting deeper and looking to counter. Their efficiency in front of goal could see them add to the scoreline if Real Sociedad overcommits. The second half could see a more open game as Real Sociedad chases the result, possibly leading to goals on both ends.
